# Copilot Coding Guidelines

## General Rules

- Write simple code
- Avoid over engineering
- Avoid unnecessary abstractions
- Prefer fewer functions
- Prefer readability over reusability
- Do not create utility files unless necessary
- Do not split logic into many files
- Follow existing codebase patterns

## Function Rules

- Avoid creating small helper functions
- Inline simple logic
- Prefer 1–3 functions per file
- Avoid deep nesting

## Architecture Rules

- Do not create classes unless necessary
- Prefer functional style
- Avoid premature optimization

## Naming

- Use short meaningful names
- Avoid overly verbose naming

## Commenting

- Sometimes add short comments wherever seems necessary
